name: Gyros (Meat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Include some fat (8 or more slices of blanched bacon or fresh pork belly, if available).
2. Grind lamb and beef together using a large plate on the meat grinder.
3. Regrind with a small plate.
4. Reconstitute onions in water in a small bowl.
5. Combine salt, sugar, oregano, black pepper, cumin, hot paprika, and cinnamon in another small bowl; mix thoroughly.
6. In a large bowl, combine the meat, rehydrated onions, and spice mixture. Add garlic and vegetable oil; mix with hands until spices are evenly distributed. Refrigerate, covered, for several hours.
7. In a small skillet, fry a small amount of the meat mixture in a little butter. Taste and adjust seasoning if desired.
8. Blanch bacon by simmering in hot water for about 3 minutes. If pork belly is used, blanching is not necessary. Drain on paper towels.
9. Line an 8 1/2 x 5 x 3" loaf pan with bacon or pork slices, covering sides and bottom. Pack gyros meat into the pan, filling to the top of the pan. Cover with remaining slices of bacon or pork.
10. Place the filled pan in a hot water bath reaching three-quarters of the way up the loaf pan. Bake at 375°F for 20-30 minutes.
11. Reduce heat to 325°F and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes.
12. Remove the pan from the oven; take it out of the hot water bath and place on a wire rack to cool. Cover with aluminum foil and weigh down with about 2 lbs to compress the loaf. Cool to room temperature and refrigerate, covered, for 12 hours or overnight to ripen and increase flavor.
13. Slice thinly and serve in pita bread with sliced onions, tomatoes, and tzatziki sauce.
